trade.summary: Modest bilateral trade. Brazil exports soybeans, coffee, iron ore, and aircraft components. Sweden exports telecommunications equipment (Ericsson), industrial machinery, vehicles (Volvo, Scania), and pharmaceuticals.

diplomatic.summary: Friendly diplomatic relations. Sweden has been a vocal supporter of Amazon conservation and contributed to the Amazon Fund. Regular bilateral consultations on climate, trade, and development cooperation. Sweden was an early partner in environmental governance discussions.

military.summary: No military alliance. Very limited defense cooperation. Sweden's traditionally neutral stance (recently changed with NATO accession) limited military ties.

financial.summary: Swedish companies (Ericsson, Volvo, Scania, IKEA, H&M) have significant Brazilian operations. Swedish FDI in telecom, automotive, and retail sectors is notable.

energy.summary: Limited direct energy trade. Sweden has supported Brazilian renewable energy projects through development cooperation. Some interest in Brazilian green hydrogen exports.

scientific.summary: Active scientific collaboration particularly in environmental science, sustainability, and life sciences. Karolinska Institute partners with Brazilian medical schools. SIDA supports research programs.
